Compute Cell State Colocalization Matrix

Source: R/Colocalization.R
dot-colocalization.Rd

This internal function computes a cell state–level colocalization matrix based on spatial proximity of single cells. It first constructs a cell–cell neighborhood graph using k-nearest neighbors within a specified radius, then aggregates neighbor relationships to quantify interactions between cell states.

Usage

.colocalization(
  scmeta,
  coords = c("X", "Y"),
  CellState = "CellState",
  radius = 50,
  k = 200,
  min.cell = 10
)

Arguments

scmeta

A data.frame containing single-cell metadata. Each row represents a cell. Row names should correspond to unique cell IDs.

coords

Character vector specifying column names in scmeta for spatial coordinates (e.g., c("X", "Y")).

CellState

Character. Column name in scmeta specifying cell state annotations (e.g., "SE1_CD4T").

radius

Numeric. Maximum Euclidean distance threshold (in the same units as X and Y) used to define neighboring cells. Default is 50.

k

Integer. Number of nearest neighbors queried per cell before applying the distance filter. Default is 200.

min.cell

Integer. Minimum number of neighboring cells required for a cell to be retained in the analysis. Cells with fewer neighbors are excluded to reduce noise. Default is 10.

Value

A numeric matrix of size (number of cell states × number of cell states), where each entry represents the normalized colocalization index between two cell states.

Details

The function performs the following steps:

  • Identifies spatial neighbors using k-nearest neighbors within a specified radius

  • Constructs a sparse binary cell–cell adjacency matrix

  • Maps cells to their corresponding cell states

  • Aggregates neighbor relationships to compute cell state–cell state interactions

  • Normalizes interactions at both the cell and cell state levels

Cells with fewer than min.cell neighbors are excluded to ensure robustness of the colocalization estimates.
